note: Every claim carries all five invariants. scripts/check-admissibility-registry.mjs resolves each verifier_behavior and negative_vector against the tree and fails the build on any gap. Claims that cannot yet satisfy all five (e.g. gate-layer reporting artifacts pending a negative conformance vector) are NOT listed until they can.

claims.claim: A named human with a device-bound passkey approved this exact action under user verification (biometric or PIN).
claims.pinned_authority: The approver's credential public key, pinned from server-side enrollment (approver_credentials). Never a key carried in the receipt, and the pinned key's class governs the verification routine.

negative_vector.case: pinned Class-A key presented with a key_class:B bare signature (downgrade) is refused

claims.acceptance_semantics: VERIFIED = the WebAuthn assertion and signature chain check out. ACCEPTED = the verifying key was pinned from enrollment and its pinned class was enforced, so a receipt-supplied or downgraded key can never elevate to Class-A.

claims.claim: Scoped human authority was valid for this exact action at authorization time: the subject held an active, in-window, non-revoked authority whose role, action scope, amount ceiling (in the stated currency), delegation chain, and pinned policy all admit this action.
claims.pinned_authority: The organization's authority registry, committed as (authority_registry_epoch, authority_registry_head) and bound into the receipt. For portable acceptance without trusting EP's live database, the relying party pins the registry issuer key out of band and accepts an EP-AUTHORITY-PROOF-v1 signed under it; the receipt's epoch/head let the relying party refuse a stale or equivocating registry.

negative_vector.case: an unknown, revoked, expired, not-yet-valid, out-of-scope, wrong-role, over-limit, currency-mismatched, policy-mismatched, delegation-broken, or unresolved-registry authority is refused (verdict is never 'authorized'), and an EP-AUTHORITY-PROOF-v1 signed by an unpinned registry key, or with a mismatched head/epoch, is not accepted

claims.parity_exception: JS reference implementation shipped first (same land-grab sequencing as EP-AEC and EP-ADMISSIBILITY, which also landed JS-first). The resolver and proof verifier are pure and canonicalize byte-identically to the JS trust-receipt verifier; Python/Go ports are a tracked fast-follow. Until they land, this claim is asserted for the JS verifier only.
claims.acceptance_semantics: VERIFIED = the resolver returns a closed verdict (one of AUTHORITY_VERDICTS) over the registry snapshot it was handed, and any EP-AUTHORITY-PROOF-v1 signature checks out. ACCEPTED = that snapshot is the org's real registry — the relying party pinned the registry issuer key out of band, and the receipt's authority_registry_epoch/head meet the relying party's freshness pin. A closed verdict against an unpinned or stale registry is verified, not accepted.

claims.claim: This evidence packet is admissible for reliance under a relying-party-pinned profile: identity, a device-bound ceremony, scoped authority, policy match, revocation freshness, and one-time consumption all compose to a single closed reliance verdict, and only 'rely' authorizes another party to act.
claims.pinned_authority: The relying party's OWN EP-RELIANCE-PROFILE-v1 — the assurance it demands, the registry issuer keys (accepted_registry_keys) and transparency issuer keys (accepted_issuer_keys) it pins, the policy hashes it accepts, the revocation freshness it requires, and the evidence it requires. EMILIA authors none of the bar and is never in the trust path; the relying party pins every root out of band.

negative_vector.case: a packet that is unsigned, from an untrusted issuer, lacking the required Class-A or quorum ceremony, with missing/revoked/expired/out-of-scope/over-limit/policy-mismatched authority, with a stale revocation check, an already-consumed authorization, or an unpinned registry key each returns the corresponding do_not_rely_* verdict and never 'rely'

claims.parity_exception: JS reference implementation shipped first (same land-grab sequencing as EP-AUTHORITY-REGISTRY and EP-AEC). The kernel is pure and composes the already-tri-language verifiers (verifyTrustReceipt, verifyQuorum, verifyRevocation, verifyConsumptionProof) plus the JS authority-proof port; Python/Go ports of evaluateReliance are a tracked fast-follow. Until they land, this claim is asserted for the JS kernel only.
claims.acceptance_semantics: VERIFIED = every composed leg (receipt signature, ceremony, authority proof, revocation, consumption) checks out under the offline verifiers. ACCEPTED = 'rely', which additionally requires those results to satisfy the relying party's OWN pinned EP-RELIANCE-PROFILE-v1 — its assurance floor, its pinned registry/issuer keys, its accepted policy hashes, its freshness bound. A packet that VERIFIES but does not meet the pinned profile is do_not_rely, not rely: reliance is the relying party's decision, never EMILIA's.

claims.claim: M distinct named humans, none of them the initiator, each bound their own device assertion to the same exact action within a time window.
claims.pinned_authority: The quorum policy from an org-pinned template keyed by (org, action_type) (lib/guard-quorum-template.js), and each approver_public_key from enrollment. Both sourced out of band, never from the quorum document.

negative_vector.case: initiator counted as an approver, and one device key filling two seats when distinct_humans is disabled, are both refused

claims.acceptance_semantics: VERIFIED = the members are internally consistent with the policy and keys handed to the verifier (threshold, distinct humans, distinct keys, initiator excluded, roster, window, order). ACCEPTED = that policy is the org's real one from the pinned template and the keys came from enrollment. Internal consistency against creator-declared inputs is not authorization.

claims.claim: The authority, delegation, or key backing this action is still current within a stated freshness bound.
claims.pinned_authority: The revocation authority's signing key, pinned by the relying party. The staleness bound itself is a relying-party policy input, graded per consequence.

negative_vector.case: a revoked target and a stale/expired revocation statement fail safe rather than open

claims.acceptance_semantics: VERIFIED = the revocation statement is authentic under the pinned authority key. ACCEPTED = the relying party's own staleness bound is met; stale material fails closed, never open.

claims.claim: This receipt existed no later than a specific time, proven by an RFC 3161 token from a trusted timestamp authority.
claims.pinned_authority: The TSA's public key, pinned by the relying party. An unpinned or unknown TSA is refused.

negative_vector.case: a token from an unpinned TSA and a messageImprint that does not match the receipt digest are refused

claims.acceptance_semantics: VERIFIED = the RFC 3161 token is well-formed and its messageImprint matches the receipt digest. ACCEPTED = the token was issued by a TSA the relying party pinned; time from an untrusted TSA carries no weight.
